growth_records.cite: Cho et al. Metabolic engineering of Corynebacterium glutamicum for the production of pyrone and pyridine dicarboxylic acids. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2024). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2415213121. [PMC11551391]
growth_records.snippet: the PSE6 strain harboring plasmids pP3 and pP1-0160 produced 76.17 ± 1.24 g/L of PDC with a yield of 0.36 ± 0.04 mol/mol glucose and a productivity of 0.63 ± 0.01 g/L/h
growth_records.notes: Final optimized PDC run. Reported: titer 76.17 g/L, yield 0.36 mol/mol glucose, productivity 0.63 g/L/h. Glucose derived: MW PDC=184.10, glucose=180.16; consumed=(76.17/184.10)/0.36*180.16=207.0 g/L. Run time=76.17/0.63=120.9 h. Volumetric qGlc=207.0/120.9=1.71 g/L/h. Full medium composition in SI Appendix (not in article text).

E. coli MG1655 K12 in M9 was also mentioned in the same case study showing residual growth without carbon source, but NO numeric growth rate was reported for E. coli (unpublished data), so no E. coli record is emitted. NOTE: PMC3765440 (S. cerevisiae), PMC5478895 (Yarrowia lipolytica), PMC10218636 (S. cerevisiae Ethanol Red) are all EUKARYOTIC yeasts and yielded no prokaryote records despite reporting chemostat/qS/qethanol data.
